ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather event recording */,
  "julian_date" INTEGER /* Julian date of the recording */,
  "event_date" TEXT /* Date of the weather event */,
  "event_time" TEXT /* Time of the weather event */,
  "altitude" REAL /* Altitude at which the measurements were taken (in meters) */,
  "wind_direction" INTEGER /* Wind direction (in degrees) */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Wind speed (in meters per second) */,
  "wind_shear" REAL /* Wind shear value (in meters per second) */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Air temperature (in degrees Celsius) */,
  "dew_point" REAL /* Dew point temperature (in degrees Celsius) */,
  "pressure" REAL /* Atmospheric pressure (in hPa) */,
  "relative_humidity" REAL /* Relative humidity (in percentage) */,
  "absolute_humidity" REAL /* Absolute humidity (in grams per cubic meter) */,
  "density" REAL /* Density of the air (in kg/m³) */,
  "index_of_refraction" REAL /* Index of refraction of air */,
  "velocity_of_sound" REAL /* Velocity of sound in air (in meters per second) */,
  "saturation_vapor_pressure" REAL /* Saturation vapor pressure (in hPa) */,
  "precipitable_water" REAL /* Total precipitable water (in mm) */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the station where the event was recorded */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographic latitude of the observation point */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographic longitude of the observation point */,
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather condition */,
  "quality_flag" TEXT /* Quality flag (e.g., pass, fail) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the observation station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the station (e.g., city, state, country) */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the station above sea level (in meters) */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the station administrator */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographic latitude of the station */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographic longitude of the station */,
  "time_zone" TEXT /* Time zone of the station */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the station was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance performed on the station */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current operational status of the station (e.g., active, maintenance, decommissioned) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if the user account is active */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or team the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather event being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the weather event */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the weather event was accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the weather event was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client accessing the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES weather_events ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_conditions" (
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather condition */,
  "condition_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather condition (e.g., Clear, Cloudy) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the weather condition */,
  "icon_url" TEXT /* URL to an icon representing the weather condition */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the weather condition (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("condition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., Remote Sensor, Manual Observations) */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of data source (e.g., Automated, Manual) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the data collection instruments */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the data collection instruments */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the data source was installed */,
  "last_calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of the last calibration of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_anomalies" (
  "anomaly_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each anomaly */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated weather event */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the anomaly */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the anomaly (e.g., Low, Medium, High) */,
  "response_action" TEXT /* Recommended response action for the anomaly */,
  "detected_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user or system that detected the anomaly */,
  "detected_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the anomaly was detected */,
  "resolved_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the anomaly was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the anomaly */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("anomaly_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_anomalies_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_anomalies_detected_by FOREIGN KEY ("detected_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_anomalies_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES weather_events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_forecasts" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast entry */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the observation station the forecast is for */,
  "forecast_date" TEXT /* Date of the forecast */,
  "forecast_time" TEXT /* Time of the forecast */,
  "temperature_forecast" REAL /* Forecasted temperature (in degrees Celsius) */,
  "precipitation_forecast" REAL /* Forecasted precipitation (in mm) */,
  "wind_speed_forecast" REAL /* Forecasted wind speed (in meters per second) */,
  "forecast_model" TEXT /* Name of the model used to generate the forecast */,
  "confidence_score" REAL /* Confidence score of the forecast (e.g., 0.85) */,
  "forecast_source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source used for the forecast */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_forecasts_forecast_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("forecast_source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_forecasts_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated weather event */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., Warning, Advisory) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "issued_date" TEXT /* Date the alert was issued */,
  "alert_level" INTEGER /* Level of the alert (e.g., 1, 2, 3) */,
  "alert_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the alert (e.g., 24 hours) */,
  "alert_status" TEXT /* Current status of the alert (e.g., active, resolved) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES weather_events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_quality_checks" (
  "check_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality check */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather event being checked */,
  "check_type" TEXT /* Type of quality check performed */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the quality check (e.g., Pass, Fail) */,
  "remarks" TEXT /* Additional remarks about the quality check */,
  "checked_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the quality check */,
  "checked_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the quality check was performed */,
  "next_check_due" TEXT /* Date and time when the next quality check is due */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("check_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_quality_checks_checked_by FOREIGN KEY ("checked_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_quality_checks_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES weather_events ("event_id")
)
